articles.summary: Investors may be taking issue with various elements of the software company’s margin forecast.

articles.title: IBM’s stock falls as software revenue underwhelms
articles.summary: Investors have been concerned about AI’s impact on IBM’s software and consulting businesses.

articles.title: Lululemon is getting a Nike veteran as its new CEO. An analyst says that could be a problem.
articles.summary: Yoga-wear maker Lululemon on Wednesday said its board had approved Nike veteran Heidi O’Neill as its new CEO. But analysts said it was too early to tell if that was the right call, and said questions about Nike’s current struggles could spill over into her new job.

articles.title: CSX says more businesses are shipping via rail to avoid surging fuel costs
articles.summary: Shares of CSX jumped after hours on Wednesday after the railroad giant raised its sales outlook for the year, as more businesses choose rail to save on fuel costs in the wake of the Iran war.

articles.title: How Trump's psychedelics executive order could unlock stalled cannabis reform
articles.summary: The White House psychedelics executive order accelerates research, clinical trials and "Right to Try" access for drugs like psilocybin, MDMA and ibogaine.

articles.title: Hormuz disruptions hit China’s Christmas capital — and holiday spending
articles.summary: Manufacturers told CNBC that shoppers will likely have to pay higher prices to match rising costs of plastic and other materials.

articles.title: Rivian's factory damaged by tornado amid crucial R2 EV launch
Sandwich chain Jersey Mike's confidentially files for IPO
articles.summary: Blackstone bought a majority stake in the sandwich chain in 2024 in a deal that valued the company at roughly $8 billion.

articles.title: Eli Lilly agrees to acquire cancer drug maker Kelonia in deal worth up to $7 billion
articles.summary: Kelonia is developing technology to reprogram patients' T-cells inside the body so those cells can attack cancer, called in vivo CAR-T.
